negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing startup rejection/panic when CHAIN_HALT_THRESHOLD_SECS >= txpool.max_tx_lifetime; no chain-halt threshold input or validation path exists in txpool maintenance.
related_but_insufficient_code: MaintainPoolConfig has max_tx_lifetime and async lifetime, but no chain-halt threshold setting or startup validation. Node builder/CLI wire general txpool lifetime without comparing against chain-halt threshold.

notes: Confirmed gap; chain-halt scheduled machinery and guard are omitted.
rationale: Both threshold candidates express the same current startup guard: poison detection must be configured to fire before ordinary pool lifetime expiry can mask it.
statement: At current tip, transaction-pool maintenance must reject or panic on startup configuration where CHAIN_HALT_THRESHOLD_SECS is greater than or equal to txpool.max_tx_lifetime, because poison detection must precede lifetime expiry.
suggested_falsification_tests: Configure CHAIN_HALT_THRESHOLD_SECS equal to or greater than txpool.max_tx_lifetime and assert maintain_transaction_pool fails before the maintenance loop starts.
Configure CHAIN_HALT_THRESHOLD_SECS below txpool.max_tx_lifetime and assert maintenance initializes.
summary: Chain-halt threshold before transaction lifetime guard is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Configure threshold >= --txpool.lifetime and start txpool maintenance; current port has no such threshold/validation path.
title: Chain-halt threshold precedes transaction lifetime
